prompt: Using RFdiffusion with symmetric diffusion, design a de novo 24-mer self-assembling protein nanocage (subunit 100–130 residues) inspired by the ferritin L-chain architecture with modified surface chemistry for antigen display and controlled drug loading. The nanocage should assemble spontaneously in solution with a diameter of 10–12 nm. outline a methodology for: (1) RFdiffusion symmetric backbone generation under O symmetry constraints, (2) ProteinMPNN sequence design for inter-subunit interface optimisation, (3) SEC-MALS and cryo-EM validation of assembly. Provide guidance on conjugation chemistry for surface antigen attachment.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: Ferritin light chain; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: iron storage nanocage (24-mer self-assembling protein shell)
